evals.name: full-feature-track
evals.prompt: I have a PRD for a TaskFlow API at tests/vbounce-e2e/workspace/current/prd.md. Start a vbounce SDLC cycle and run through the full Feature Track — from Requirements to Deployment. For EACH phase, follow the complete 6-Activity Phase Anatomy: (1) INPUT, (2) AI GENERATION via the phase agent, (3) QUALITY GATE via quality-gate-validator agent (separate invocation, not self-check), (4) present for HUMAN REVIEW, (5) REFINEMENT if needed, (6) APPROVAL triggers traceability-analyst update + knowledge-curator capture. Use APPROVED to advance each phase after quality gate passes. Save all artifacts to the workspace directory.
evals.expected_output: Complete 6-phase cycle with artifacts at each phase, separate quality gate verdicts from quality-gate-validator agent, traceability matrix updates at each transition, knowledge capture entries after each approval. Final e2e-report.md with per-phase PASS/FAIL including anatomy step verification.

evals.name: requirements-phase-full-anatomy
evals.prompt:
I have a PRD at tests/vbounce-e2e/workspace/current/prd.md for a task management API. Start a vbounce SDLC cycle and run the Requirements phase following the COMPLETE 6-Activity Phase Anatomy:

Step 1 (INPUT): Load the PRD context.
Step 2 (AI GENERATION): Use the requirements-analyst agent to produce structured user stories (US-XXX-XXX), acceptance criteria (GIVEN-WHEN-THEN, AC-XXX, >=3 per story), NFRs (NFR-XXX-XXX with measurable thresholds), test skeletons (T-AC-XXX), traceability matrix, and ambiguity report (all scores < 50).
Step 3 (QUALITY GATE): Invoke the quality-gate-validator agent SEPARATELY to produce a YAML verdict (QG-REQ-*) with per-criterion PASS/WARN/FAIL. This must be a distinct agent invocation, not a self-check inside requirements.
Step 4 (HUMAN REVIEW): Present the artifacts and QG verdict for my review.
Step 5 (REFINEMENT): I may request changes — if so, revise and re-run QG.
Step 6 (APPROVAL): When I say APPROVED, invoke traceability-analyst to update the matrix and knowledge-curator to capture requirements phase learnings.

Save all artifacts to the workspace.
evals.expected_output: requirements.md, test-skeletons.md, traceability.md, ambiguity-report.md with correct vbounce ID formats. PLUS: separate QG verdict YAML from quality-gate-validator. PLUS: traceability update confirmation. PLUS: knowledge capture entry for requirements phase.

evals.name: quality-gate-standalone
evals.prompt: Run the vbounce quality gate validator on the requirements output in tests/vbounce-e2e/workspace/current/. I want to see a PASS/WARN/FAIL verdict with per-criterion results covering: completeness, ambiguity scores, NFR coverage, testability, story independence, and traceability. Output must be YAML format with quality_gate_id, per-criterion status/threshold/actual, blocking_issues, and recommendation.
evals.expected_output: YAML-formatted quality gate report with quality_gate_id: QG-REQ-*, verdict (PASS/WARN/FAIL), per-criterion status with threshold vs actual, blocking_issues list, and recommendation (proceed_to_review or revise_and_recheck).

evals.name: refinement-loop
evals.prompt: Continue the vbounce requirements phase. I've reviewed the requirements and I have feedback: CHANGES REQUESTED: (1) Add edge case ACs for webhook retry failures when the target server returns 5xx errors, (2) NFR for webhook payload size limit is missing, (3) US-003-002 needs an AC for HMAC signature verification by the receiver. Please revise the requirements and re-run the quality gate.
evals.expected_output: Revised requirements.md with new ACs added, new NFR for payload size, updated AC for HMAC verification. Quality-gate-validator re-invoked on revised output with new QG verdict. Traceability matrix updated to reflect new ACs and tests.

evals.name: design-phase-full-anatomy
evals.prompt:
Continue the vbounce SDLC cycle. Requirements are APPROVED. Run the Design phase following the COMPLETE 6-Activity Phase Anatomy:

Step 1 (INPUT): Verify all required input files exist — requirements.md, test-skeletons.md, traceability.md, state.yaml.
Step 2 (AI GENERATION): Use the design-architect agent to produce ALL 8 output files:
  - design.md — component diagrams and sequence diagrams in Mermaid (NO ASCII art)
  - security-design.md — full STRIDE analysis (S/T/R/I/D/E) for EVERY component and data flow, each threat with severity + mitigation
  - api-spec.md — every US-* maps to >= 1 endpoint, with method/path/DTOs/auth/errors
  - database-schema.md — ER diagram in Mermaid, migration plan
  - architecture-decisions.md — >= 1 ADR with >= 2 alternatives considered per decision
  - traceability.md — REQ->Story->API->Component->STRIDE->ADR mapping
  - test-impact.md — unit/integration/security test plan per component
  - test-specifications.md — ITS-* (integration), STS-* (system), SECTS-* (security) specs detailed enough for testing agent
Step 3 (QUALITY GATE): Invoke quality-gate-validator agent SEPARATELY with phase=design. Must produce qg-design.yaml with QG-DES-* ID.
Step 4 (HUMAN REVIEW): Present artifacts summary, QG verdict, key ADRs, and architecture overview for my review.
Step 5 (REFINEMENT): I may request changes.
Step 6 (APPROVAL): When I say APPROVED, invoke traceability-analyst (Update mode) and knowledge-curator (Per-Phase capture).

Save all artifacts to the workspace design/ directory.
evals.expected_output: 8 design files with Mermaid diagrams, STRIDE per component, ITS/STS/SECTS specs, ADRs with alternatives. Separate QG verdict YAML (qg-design.yaml). Traceability update + knowledge capture on approval.

evals.name: implementation-phase-full-anatomy
evals.prompt:
Continue the vbounce SDLC cycle. Design is APPROVED. Run the Implementation phase (FAST-TRACK mode) following the COMPLETE 6-Activity Phase Anatomy:

Step 1 (INPUT): Verify input files — design.md, api-spec.md, database-schema.md, security-design.md, test-skeletons.md, state.yaml.
Step 2 (AI GENERATION): Use the implementation-engineer agent to produce:
  - Source code in project src/ directories following the approved design
  - summary.md — lists ALL files created/modified with line counts
  - package-verification.md — EVERY package/import verified against real registries. ZERO hallucinated packages.
  - tests-created.md — test skeletons from requirements instantiated as runnable tests
  Design conformance: code structure must match approved architecture (controllers->services->repositories).
Step 3 (QUALITY GATE): Invoke quality-gate-validator agent SEPARATELY with phase=implementation. Must produce qg-implementation.yaml. Key criteria: 0 hallucinated packages, test presence, design conformance.
Step 4 (HUMAN REVIEW): Present implementation summary, package verification results, and QG verdict.
Step 5 (REFINEMENT): I may request changes.
Step 6 (APPROVAL): When I say APPROVED, invoke traceability-analyst + knowledge-curator.

Save artifacts to workspace implementation/ directory. Source code to project src/.
evals.expected_output: Source code matching design, summary.md, package-verification.md (0 hallucinations), tests-created.md. Separate QG verdict (qg-implementation.yaml). Traceability + KC on approval.

evals.name: review-phase-full-anatomy
evals.prompt:
Continue the vbounce SDLC cycle. Implementation is APPROVED. Run the Review phase following the COMPLETE 6-Activity Phase Anatomy:

Step 1 (INPUT): Verify input files — implementation/summary.md, implementation/package-verification.md, source code (from summary), design/design.md, design/security-design.md, design/api-spec.md, state.yaml.
Step 2 (AI GENERATION): Use the review-auditor agent to produce ALL 3 output files:
  - review-report.md — 5-category scored review: (1) Hallucination Detection, (2) Security Audit, (3) Code Quality, (4) Logic Correctness, (5) Performance. Each category scored 0-100 with weighted overall verdict.
  - hallucination-report.md — EVERY package, API call, and config option verified against real documentation.
  - security-findings.md — STRIDE mitigations from design verified in implementation code.
  File coverage: EVERY implementation file must appear in the review report.
Step 3 (QUALITY GATE): Invoke quality-gate-validator agent SEPARATELY with phase=review. Must produce qg-review.yaml.
Step 4 (HUMAN REVIEW): Present review scores, critical findings, and hallucination check results.
Step 5 (REFINEMENT): I may request changes.
Step 6 (APPROVAL): When I say APPROVED, invoke traceability-analyst + knowledge-curator.

Save all artifacts to workspace review/ directory.
evals.expected_output: review-report.md with 5-category scores, hallucination-report.md with per-package verification, security-findings.md with STRIDE verification. Separate QG verdict (qg-review.yaml). KC + traceability on approval.

evals.name: testing-phase-full-anatomy
evals.prompt:
Continue the vbounce SDLC cycle. Review is APPROVED. Run the Testing phase following the COMPLETE 6-Activity Phase Anatomy:

Step 1 (INPUT): Verify input files — requirements/requirements.md, requirements/test-skeletons.md, design/test-specifications.md, implementation/summary.md, source code, state.yaml.
Step 2 (AI GENERATION): Use the testing-engineer agent to produce:
  - Test suite in project test/ directories implementing ALL test skeletons from requirements AND design-time specs (ITS-*, STS-*, SECTS-*)
  - test-report.md — distribution stats targeting 40/20/10/10/10/10 (positive/negative/edge/security/component-integration/system-E2E), V-Model level classification (acceptance/system/integration/unit/security)
  - coverage-matrix.md — EVERY AC mapped to >= 1 test, 100% AC coverage required
  - test-results.md — pass/fail for all tests with execution output
  V-Model: tests at ALL levels (acceptance, system, integration, unit, security).
Step 3 (QUALITY GATE): Invoke quality-gate-validator agent SEPARATELY with phase=testing. Must produce qg-testing.yaml. Key criteria: distribution within tolerance, 100% AC coverage, all V-Model levels present, ITS/STS/SECTS implemented.
Step 4 (HUMAN REVIEW): Present test distribution, coverage stats, V-Model breakdown, and QG verdict.
Step 5 (REFINEMENT): I may request changes.
Step 6 (APPROVAL): When I say APPROVED, invoke traceability-analyst + knowledge-curator.

Save artifacts to workspace testing/ directory. Test code to project test/.
evals.expected_output: Test suite implementing all skeletons + design specs. test-report.md with distribution + V-Model. coverage-matrix.md with 100% AC coverage. test-results.md. Separate QG verdict (qg-testing.yaml). KC + traceability on approval.

evals.name: deployment-phase-full-anatomy
evals.prompt:
Continue the vbounce SDLC cycle. Testing is APPROVED. Run the Deployment phase following the COMPLETE 6-Activity Phase Anatomy:

Step 1 (INPUT): Verify input files — testing/test-results.md, testing/coverage-matrix.md, requirements/requirements.md, implementation/summary.md, state.yaml.
Step 2 (AI GENERATION): Use the deployment-engineer agent to produce ALL 4 output files:
  - deployment-plan.md — pre-deployment checklist, deployment steps, post-deployment verification. Must include rollback triggers with QUANTITATIVE thresholds (error rate %, latency ms, not vague 'if problems occur').
  - acceptance-verification.md — load ALL ACs from requirements, map to test results via traceability, verify 100% AC coverage with passing tests. Any AC without passing test = BLOCK.
  - pre-deploy-checklist.md — all items checkable (not vague), includes security sign-off, DB migration verified, monitoring configured.
  - monitoring.md — alert thresholds defined with specific numbers (error rate, latency p95/p99, CPU/memory), notification channels, escalation procedures.
Step 3 (QUALITY GATE): Invoke quality-gate-validator agent SEPARATELY with phase=deployment. Must produce qg-deployment.yaml.
Step 4 (HUMAN REVIEW): Present acceptance verification result, rollback triggers, monitoring thresholds, and QG verdict.
Step 5 (REFINEMENT): I may request changes.
Step 6 (APPROVAL): When I say APPROVED, invoke knowledge-curator in End-of-Cycle mode for full retrospective.

Save all artifacts to workspace deployment/ directory.
evals.expected_output: 4 deployment files with quantitative rollback triggers, 100% acceptance verification, monitoring thresholds. Separate QG verdict (qg-deployment.yaml). End-of-cycle KC retrospective on approval.

evals.name: cross-phase-verification
evals.prompt:
The full vbounce SDLC cycle is complete (Requirements through Deployment all APPROVED). Run cross-phase verification:

1. TRACEABILITY CHAIN: Read the final traceability.yaml. Verify the FULL chain exists: PRD Section -> User Story -> AC -> Test Skeleton -> Design Component -> API Endpoint -> Source File -> Test File -> Deployment Check. Count orphans at each level — target is 0 orphans.

2. KNOWLEDGE CAPTURE: Check that knowledge/ directory has capture files for ALL 6 phases (requirements-capture.yaml, design-capture.yaml, implementation-capture.yaml, review-capture.yaml, testing-capture.yaml, deployment-capture.yaml). Each must contain phase-specific learnings.

3. STATE VALIDITY: Read the final state.yaml. Verify: cycle_id matches CYCLE-* format, ALL 6 phases show status: approved, history entries exist for each transition.

4. QG COMPLETENESS: Verify quality-gates/ directory has QG YAML files for all 6 phases (qg-requirements.yaml through qg-deployment.yaml), each with a verdict.

5. RETROSPECTIVE: Verify knowledge-curator produced an end-of-cycle retrospective with cross-phase patterns, prevention rules written to .claude/rules/vbounce-learned-rules.md.

Produce a cross-phase-report.md with PASS/FAIL per check.
evals.expected_output: cross-phase-report.md with: traceability chain depth + orphan count, 6/6 KC files verified, state.yaml valid with all phases approved, 6/6 QG files present, retrospective with prevention rules.
